content.method: Municipal-level cost accounting study comparing realized (not projected) per-pupil expenditure, transport subsidy outlays, and capital maintenance costs before/after closure events in a matched sample of Hungarian districts; triangulate with KLIK/tankerület budget records.
content.question: What is the net fiscal balance (savings minus transport, capital, transition, and opportunity costs) of closing versus retaining a village primary school, conditional on school size, district, and region?
content.why_it_matters: T2, T5, and T6 all rest on cost-saving assumptions that Finnish and Polish evidence suggests are often asserted but not verified; T1 and T3's viability also depend on whether retained small schools are fiscally sustainable under current formulas. Resolving this would determine whether default-closure or retention-oriented models have a real fiscal basis.
